zh.lifeSummary: 當Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg於1191年3月13日出生在德國薩辛可萊斯 萊格福洛賀包格時，他的父親Count Ludwig of Froburg II30歲，母親Richenza, Princess of Dillingen19歲。他於大約1209年與Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1259年11月3日在他的家鄉享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg于1191年3月13日出生在德国薩辛可萊斯 萊格福洛賀包格时，他的父亲Count Ludwig of Froburg II30岁，母亲Richenza, Princess of Dillingen19岁。他于大约1209年与Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1259年11月3日在他的家乡享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Ludwig
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from hlud ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’; an ancient royal and imperial name. See Louis .

variants.name: Louis
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

nameMeanings.name: III.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg was born on 13 March 1191, in Frohburg, Leipzig, Saxony, Germany, his father, Count Ludwig of Froburg II, was 30 and his mother, Richenza, Princess of Dillingen, was 19. He married Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg about 1209. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 3 November 1259, in his hometown, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg est né le 13 mars 1191, à Frohburg, Kreis Leipzig, Sachsen, Allemagne, son père, Count Ludwig of Froburg II, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Richenza, Princess of Dillingen, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg environ 1209. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 3 novembre 1259, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg am 13. März 1191 in Frohburg, Kreis Leipzig, Sachsen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Count Ludwig of Froburg II 30 und seine Mutter Richenza, Princess of Dillingen 19. Er hat um 1209 Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 3. November 1259 im Alter von 68 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg è nato il il 13 marzo 1191 nel Frohburg, Kreis Leipzig, Sachsen, Germania, il suo padre, Count Ludwig of Froburg II, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Richenza, Princess of Dillingen, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg circa 1209. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 3 novembre 1259, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburgが1191年3月13日にドイツサチセンクレイス レイプズイグフロhブルグで生まれた時，父Count Ludwig of Froburg IIは30歳，母Richenza, Princess of Dillingenは19歳であった。約1209年にGertrud Gräfin von Habsburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1259年11月3日に故郷で68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg(이)가 1191년 3월 13일 독일 작센 크레이스 레이p지그 프로h버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Count Ludwig of Froburg II(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Richenza, Princess of Dillingen(은)는 19세였다. 그는 대략 1209년 Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1259년 11월 3일 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.13. März 1191–3. November 1259: 13 de março de 1191–3 de novembro de 1259
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg nasceu em 13 de março de 1191, em Frohburg, Kreis Leipzig, Sachsen, Alemanha, seu pai, Count Ludwig of Froburg II, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Richenza, Princess of Dillingen, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg aproximadamente 1209. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 3 de novembro de 1259, em sua cidade natal, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg родился 13 марта 1191 г. в местности фрохбург, крис липзиг, сачсен, Германия, его отцу по имени Count Ludwig of Froburg II было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Richenza, Princess of Dillingen, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg около 1209 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 3 ноября 1259 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 68 лет.

es.13. März 1191–3. November 1259: 13 de marzo de 1191–3 de noviembre de 1259
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ludwig III. Graf von  Frohburg nació el 13 de marzo de 1191, en Frohburg, Kreis Leipzig, Sachsen, Alemania, su papá, Count Ludwig of Froburg II, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Richenza, Princess of Dillingen, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg aproximadamente 1209. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 3 de noviembre de 1259, en su pueblo, a la edad de 68 años.
